scenario_results.reason: Created evidence-backed handoff summary with named prior/destination owners, required artifacts, and no overreach into execution authority.
Missing readiness artifacts were surfaced and returned as a blocked-dependency escalation before any routing closure.
Duplicate owner claims were tracked in a dependency escalation note with required evidence conflicts preserved.
Seasonal and terrain-based gap was treated as a sequencing blocker with explicit requirement for weather and field-condition evidence.
Food-safety and environmental risk posture was escalated and closure was explicitly blocked until risk-specific owners respond.
Out-of-scope permit and medical/environmental signoff request was refused and routed to the appropriate adjacent owner set.
Lane correctly rejected direct execution and booking requests, returning routing confirmation for operational handlers.
Ambiguous cross-team ownership was not guessed; blocker state was preserved and escalation required before transfer.
Missing tenant context (operator and governance records) triggered low-confidence escalation with explicit assumptions log.
Conflict between public guidance and tenant policy escalated for human resolution instead of confident synthesis.
Industry terminology was normalized and mapped to a consistent handoff status artifact without false closure.
Closure confirmation was issued only after explicit destination-owner proof and completed blocker checks.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold with complete ability coverage.
